Checklist Item: Semantic-to-div ratio above threshold; pages with >70% generic containers (div/span) flagged
Comments: div/span ratio in blocks JS is ~67.7% (113/167 createElement calls), just below the 70% flag threshold. Semantic elements (a, p, li, ul, nav, button, details, dialog) are used. HTML pages (404, 418) use main, header, footer, h2 correctly.

Checklist Item: Correct heading hierarchy maintained
Comments: Heading elements h2–h6 are used across blocks. h1 is expected to come from page document content (EDS convention). h5/h6 appear in modals and navigation. No programmatic heading hierarchy enforcement; review is needed to confirm h1 exists on all pages and hierarchy is maintained in context, but block-level usage looks reasonable.

Checklist Item: Single h1 per page where applicable
Comments: No block creates multiple h1s. scripts.js uses querySelector('h1') (singular) to build the hero block, confirming single-h1-per-page expectation. h1 comes from page document content in EDS convention.

Checklist Item: DOM source order matches visual tab order; no positive tabindex values; CSS order/flex-direction does not contradict DOM sequence
Comments: Positive tabindex found: blocks/modal/modal.js sets tabindex="1" on the dialog element. All other tabindex usages are 0 or -1 (valid). Positive tabindex values disrupt natural tab order. Also tabindex=0 on drop-down items in header.js is fine. No CSS order/flex-direction issues found in JS source.

Checklist Item: Page content hierarchy intact: headings and body text present in raw HTML source; critical content not injected solely via JavaScript; no content exclusively hidden via inline style or JS-only class toggling
Comments: Static pages (404, 418) have content in raw HTML. EDS pages load content via document model (not JS-only). Some UI components use JS to toggle display:none for interactive elements (billing form, cart, gift options) but these are not critical page content. Core headings/body text are in the DOM, not exclusively JS-injected.

Checklist Item: Avoid excessive DOM depth
Comments: DOM nesting in blocks follows typical component patterns. No evidence of excessive nesting (10+ levels) from code inspection. The most complex block (product-details) builds card/carousel/modal structures with reasonable depth. EDS architecture uses flat section/block structure by default.

Checklist Item: Radio/checkbox groups wrapped in fieldset with legend; forms with 3+ related inputs have grouping
Comments: No fieldset or legend elements found anywhere in blocks JS. Radio group in canon-shipping.js (cs-addr inputs) and checkbox in lease-section.js are not wrapped in fieldset/legend. This is a violation for radio groups especially.

Checklist Item: All inputs have associated labels
Comments: Text inputs in makeTextField/makeStateSelect have properly associated labels via htmlFor. However, the radio buttons in the address book popup (canon-shipping.js) have IDs (cs-addr-N) but the label is a span without a for attribute — these radio inputs are effectively unlabeled. Lease checkbox also needs verification.

Checklist Item: Placeholder not used as label
Comments: No HTML placeholder attributes found on input elements in blocks. All references to "placeholder" are the EDS fetchPlaceholders() i18n utility. Text inputs use visible labels via htmlFor (makeTextField/makeStateSelect in canon-shipping.js). Placeholder-as-label anti-pattern not present.

Checklist Item: Error messages programmatically associated with inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage; error containers use role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'; error text is non-empty and >10 characters
Comments: Error messages are displayed via CSS class and span elements (e.g., canon-shipping.js creates a span with error text) but no aria-describedby or aria-errormessage links them to inputs. Cart/mini-cart alerts correctly use role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' for toast messages, but form validation errors are not programmatically associated with their inputs.

Checklist Item: Avoid inline styles
Comments: 39 inline style assignments in blocks JS. Most are acceptable functional patterns (display:none toggling, overflow:hidden for scroll locking, dynamic pixel heights). However, some set layout properties like justifyContent, marginTop, and paddingRight that should be in CSS classes. commerce-confirm-account.js and commerce-create-password.js are the main offenders.

Checklist Item: Accessible names for interactive elements
Comments: Most interactive elements have accessible names: buttons use textContent or aria-label (Close, Zoom image, Toggle order summary, Cart, Go back, etc). Icon-only close button in modal.js has aria-label='Close'. mobileToggle has visible text 'ACCOUNT MENU'. feedbackButton has textContent 'Feedback'. Tooltip buttons use aria-label with contextual text. No unnamed interactive elements found in custom blocks.

Checklist Item: Images with role='presentation' or aria-hidden='true' have empty alt; standalone without adjacent text and without alt flagged; hero/banner images verified to have non-empty alt >10 chars; in decorative containers verified to have empty alt or role='presentation'
Comments: Good alt text practices found. Decorative/icon images use alt="" with aria-hidden="true" (tooltip icons, pencil edit icons, banner icons). Content images in product-details use descriptive alt text (product title, product name). No standalone img elements without alt attribute found.

Checklist Item: Alt text is not a filename or path; not generic (image/photo/icon/picture/graphic/banner/logo without qualifier); between 5-125 characters; does not duplicate adjacent text content; does not begin with 'image of'/'picture of'/'photo of'; alt on linked images describes destination
Comments: All non-empty alt text values are meaningful: product names/titles from API data (pkg.name, value.title, selectedPrinter.name), product list uses label or falls back to product name. Hardcoded alt 'Shop Products' is descriptive. No filenames, generic terms, or 'image of' prefixes found. Alt lengths look reasonable (product names typically 10-80 chars).

Checklist Item: Tables use thead and tbody
Comments: No custom table elements found in blocks JS or HTML. No table, thead, or tbody created in any block. Not applicable to this codebase.

Checklist Item: Table headers use th
Comments: No custom table elements found in blocks. Not applicable.

Checklist Item: Lists use ul or ol correctly
Comments: ul/ol/li used correctly throughout. Cards block wraps card items in ul>li. Carousel slides use ul>li, indicators use ol>li. Header nav items use li. pdp-tabs uses ul>li for tab list. All list elements are properly nested.

Checklist Item: Page <title> exists; is >10 characters; not a default value (Untitled/Document/Home/Page/Website/Welcome); follows consistent pattern across site (e.g. 'Page - Site' or 'Page | Site' separator detected)
Comments: Static HTML pages have descriptive titles: "Page not found" (404.html, 15 chars) and "Invalid Site Configuration" (418.html, 26 chars). Neither is a default/generic value. EDS page titles are set via document metadata from content source. No title suffix pattern enforced in code — consistency depends on content authoring convention.

Checklist Item: Meta viewport configured correctly
Comments: All HTML files include correct viewport meta: width=device-width, initial-scale=1 (or 1.0). head.html (the EDS shared head fragment) also has this set correctly.

Checklist Item: No duplicate IDs
Comments: Potential duplicate ID: 'mini-pdp-modal' is set by both commerce-mini-cart.js and commerce-cart.js. If both blocks appear on the same page, this creates a duplicate ID. Similarly 'signin-modal'/'signin-form' appears in two blocks. Carousel, tabs, and form input IDs use dynamic/unique values. Static IDs like nav, feedbackLinkHead, comparison-modal appear once per page.

Checklist Item: No invalid HTML nesting
Comments: Reviewed all innerHTML template strings in blocks. No invalid nesting detected: div/span containers are used for block elements, anchor tags are not nested, li>a patterns are valid, p tags do not contain block elements. Static HTML pages (404, 418) have valid structure with main>div>h2/p nesting.

Checklist Item: HTML validates without errors
Comments: Both static HTML files use correct HTML5 DOCTYPE. Only validator available is a 2006 HTML Tidy (pre-HTML5) which raises false positives for semantic elements (header, main, footer, svg). No actual structural HTML errors found on manual inspection: proper nesting, closed tags, valid attributes. npm html-validate not installed; W3C validator not reachable from local audit. Marking as pass based on manual inspection.

Checklist Item: ARIA roles do not duplicate native semantics
Comments: role="dialog" on a native <dialog> element is redundant (dialog already has implicit dialog role). role="region" on div carousel is acceptable. role="tablist/tab/tabpanel" on ul/li/div is correct ARIA tab pattern. role="alert" on notification divs is correct. The redundant role="dialog" on modal.js is a minor issue but not harmful.

Checklist Item: Avoid misuse of ARIA roles
Comments: All ARIA roles are used correctly: alert on notification divs, region on carousel landmark, dialog on div popups (valid - these are div not native dialog), tablist/tab/tabpanel forming a proper tab widget. No misuse such as role="button" on non-interactive elements or role="heading" without level found.

Checklist Item: ARIA used only when necessary
Comments: ARIA usage is targeted and purposeful. aria-label (31) used where native text is absent. aria-hidden (31) marks decorative icons. aria-expanded (19) on disclosure widgets. aria-live (4) for dynamic regions. aria-modal (3) for dialogs. No excessive or unnecessary ARIA layering over elements that already have native semantics (except the noted role="dialog" on native dialog).

Checklist Item: Meta tags do not block zoom
Comments: No user-scalable=no or maximum-scale restrictions found in any meta viewport tags. All viewport meta uses only width=device-width, initial-scale=1. Zoom is not blocked.

Checklist Item: No broken links (run broken-link-checker or linkinator CLI against local build or source HTML)
Comments: No hardcoded broken links found in source. All hrefs are either root-relative (/), fragment-based (#), or dynamic runtime values. linkinator requires a running server; no local build available to crawl. No dead href literals detected in static HTML or JS templates. Cannot fully verify live links without runtime environment.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for all colors
Comments: 20 hardcoded color values found across block CSS files, not using CSS variables. Offenders include: pdp-tabs.css (#fff, #959595, #4d4d4d), product-details.css (#dbe5f7), hero.css (#d6dbe2, #424242), commerce-account-sidebar.css (#1a1a1a, #9e9b9b, #B8BECA), product-list.css (#f8f8f8), commerce-checkout.css (#5c6bc0), header.css (#d6dbe2). Overlay rgba values (rgb(0 0 0 / 50%)) and semi-transparent blacks are borderline acceptable but should use CSS vars.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for spacing scale
Comments: CSS spacing token scale is defined in styles.css (--spacing-xxsmall through --spacing-xxhuge). However, ~200 hardcoded spacing values (px/rem/em) found in block CSS files, not using these tokens. Examples: footer.css (40px, 32px, 16px), accordion.css (24px, 46px), carousel.css (68px, 92px), commerce-customer-information.css (42px, 30px, 60px). Widespread failure to adopt the spacing scale.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for typography scale
Comments: Typography tokens defined in styles.css (--font-xxs through --font-6xl, --font-weight-regular/semibold/bold). However, ~103 hardcoded typography values found in blocks, ignoring these tokens. Examples: tech-specs.css (1.68rem, 1.4rem, 2.24rem, 4.6rem), product-details.css (11px, 0.8rem), commerce-cart.css (font-weight:600 instead of var(--font-weight-semibold)). Consistent token adoption is missing.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded hex/rgb color values
Comments: 43 hardcoded hex/rgb color values found in block CSS files (same finding as row 27). Most impacted files: header.css, pdp-tabs.css, product-details.css, hero.css, commerce-account-sidebar.css, product-list.css, carousel.css, modal.css. Color tokens exist in styles.css but are not being used consistently in block-level CSS.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded pixel spacing where tokens exist
Comments: Same finding as row 28. ~200 hardcoded px/rem spacing values across blocks instead of using --spacing-* tokens. Tokens are defined (--spacing-xxsmall: 4px through --spacing-xxhuge: 192px) but widely ignored. See row 28 for full details.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Naming is predominantly BEM/kebab-case (block__element--modifier) and consistent. One violation: 'authCombineNavElement' (camelCase) used as a class in renderAuthCombine.js, breaking the kebab-case convention. CSS custom properties use consistent --property-name format. JS variables follow camelCase internally. Minor single-class inconsistency.

Checklist Item: Class names do not contain hex values or color names as identifiers; no purely positional names (left1/col2); BEM or project naming convention pattern enforced
Comments: No class names containing hex values, color names, or positional identifiers (left1/col2) found. Names like 'commerce-customer-information-row--bordered' and 'checkout__credit-application-check' are semantic. BEM/kebab-case convention followed consistently across blocks.

Checklist Item: CSS selectors for same component co-located in same file; no duplicate selectors scattered across files; related rules grouped by component
Comments: Each block has a single co-located CSS file (one CSS per block directory). No block styles found scattered across global stylesheets. No duplicate selectors across files for the same component. EDS architecture enforces this pattern by design.
Evidence: blocks/ directory structure — one .css per block

Checklist Item: Layout containers use display:flex or display:grid; no float:left/right for multi-column layouts; no display:table for non-tabular content; absolute positioning in <5% of layout containers
Comments: Layout uses flex (208 instances) and grid across blocks — no float:left/right or display:table for layout. 48 position:absolute instances exist (~11.6% of display declarations) but inspection shows these are all legitimate UI overlay uses: carousel nav arrows, accordion expand icon, modal close button, dropdown menus, hero text overlay, loading spinner. None are multi-column layout containers.

Checklist Item: min-width media queries outnumber max-width queries; base styles (outside media queries) target mobile viewport dimensions
Comments: Mobile-first approach confirmed. 44 min-width media queries vs only 3 max-width queries across blocks and styles CSS. Base styles outside media queries target mobile viewport. Strong mobile-first implementation.

Checklist Item: Standard breakpoints only
Comments: Breakpoints are mostly standard: 900px (primary), 768px, 600px, 1200px, 1024px. Minor deviations: header.css uses max-width:899px (companion to 900px min-width boundary), max-height:819px (height query — legitimate for fixed-height UI), and checkout-success.css uses min-width:320px/max-width:768px range. No arbitrary one-off breakpoints for content.

Checklist Item: Responsive images by default
Comments: Responsive images implemented at multiple levels: EDS createOptimizedPicture() generates picture elements with srcset and webp sources; styles.css sets main img { max-width: 100% } globally. Product images use explicit width/height attributes. Strong responsive image implementation.

Checklist Item: Avoid z-index escalation
Comments: Z-index escalation found. Values range from 0–9999 with arbitrary jumps: commerce-account-sidebar uses 9999 (panel) and 9998 (overlay), header uses 9999 for nav. No CSS custom properties or z-index scale used. Values like 9999/9998 are a sign of escalation workarounds. Checkout uses 1000 for a dialog overlay. No tokens for layering management.

Checklist Item: Avoid expensive box-shadows
Comments: Box shadows are minimal and performant: small blur radii (2–12px), low opacity (8–18%). No large spread values or multiple stacked shadows. Two blocks use CSS token var(--shape-shadow-2). Box shadow transitions exist but are short (0.15–0.2s ease). No expensive filter:drop-shadow found.

Checklist Item: Avoid heavy CSS filters
Comments: Only two CSS filters used: filter:invert(1) on a single icon in product-details (simple inversion), filter:grayscale(100%) on product-list images (single function). No backdrop-filter, blur(), or multi-function filter chains. No expensive filter usage detected.

Checklist Item: No infinite CSS animations on non-loading elements; animation duration under 1s; total animation count per page within threshold
Comments: Infinite animations are loading-state only: spinners (pdp-spin 0.7s, product-list-spin 0.8s — both under 1s threshold) and skeleton shimmers (1.4s — slightly over 1s but skeleton loaders are an accepted exception). Nav slide-in is 0.25s one-shot. No infinite animations on persistent non-loading elements. Total of 6 animation declarations — well within threshold.

Checklist Item: All CSS properties/values supported in target browsers (checked against caniuse compatibility data); no unprefixed experimental features without fallbacks
Comments: Modern CSS selectors :has() and :is() used in several blocks — broadly supported (Chrome 105+, Safari 15.4+, Firefox 121+). No fallbacks provided but audience likely uses modern browsers. Vendor prefixes used correctly for scrollbar styling (-webkit-scrollbar, -ms-overflow-style) and touch (-webkit-overflow-scrolling, -webkit-tap-highlight-color). No unprefixed experimental features without fallbacks detected.

Checklist Item: Avoid vendor prefixes unless required
Comments: Vendor prefixes are limited to necessary cases: -webkit- prefixes for scrollbar styling, -webkit-overflow-scrolling (iOS momentum scroll), -webkit-tap-highlight-color, -webkit-details-marker. -ms-overflow-style used for scrollbar hiding (IE/legacy Edge). No unnecessary -webkit- or -moz- prefixes for properties now universally supported without prefix (e.g. transform, flexbox, animation).

Checklist Item: No duplicated CSS rules
Comments: Duplicate CSS selectors found in 10 block CSS files. Worst offenders: commerce-checkout.css (11 duplicates including .checkout__aside, .checkout__content, canon-shipping selectors), login.css (4 duplicates), product-list.css, header.css, columns.css (2 each). Some may be intentional responsive overrides inside media queries, but bare duplicates outside queries represent redundant rules.

Checklist Item: No unused CSS selectors
Comments: PurgeCSS analysis: accordion.css loses ~4 lines (minimal), product-details.css loses ~259 lines when scoped to JS only. However EDS blocks receive dynamic content from authored documents so PurgeCSS against JS alone over-reports unused selectors. No obviously dead/commented-out CSS rule blocks found on manual inspection. Cannot conclusively flag unused selectors without full page rendering.

Checklist Item: Consistent property ordering
Comments: No CSS property ordering convention enforced. stylelintrc uses stylelint-config-standard which does not include property ordering rules. Properties in block CSS files follow no consistent order — positioning mixed with box model, typography mixed with layout. No stylelint-config-idiomatic-css or similar ordering plugin configured.

Checklist Item: No blocking scripts in head
Comments: All scripts in head use type="module" (inherently deferred/non-blocking) or are importmaps/speculationrules (non-executable). No classic blocking <script src="..."> tags in head. scripts.js, aem.js, and commerce.js all loaded as modules. Full compliance.

Checklist Item: Use async or defer appropriately
Comments: All HTML-declared scripts use type="module" (inherently deferred). Dynamically injected scripts via aem.js loadScript() are appended to DOM (async by default). Verint third-party script loads on 3s setTimeout delay. No render-blocking async=false patterns. commerce.js and external-asset-utils.js use dynamic injection patterns which are non-blocking.

Checklist Item: Third-party scripts lazy loaded
Comments: Third-party scripts are properly lazy loaded. Analytics SDK (commerce-events-sdk.js, commerce-events-collector.js) loads via scripts/delayed.js which is imported after a 3000ms setTimeout in scripts.js. Verint script also uses 3s setTimeout delay. Standard EDS delayed loading pattern implemented correctly.

Checklist Item: No JS files loaded on pages where their exports are unused; code-split bundles match route usage
Comments: EDS architecture loads block JS on-demand: loadSections() dynamically imports each block's JS only when that block exists in the page DOM. Header/footer conditionally skipped via no-header/no-footer meta tags. Commerce initializers loaded per page type. No monolithic bundle loading unused code. Route-level code splitting is implicit through EDS block-based architecture.

Checklist Item: No marketing tags in head
Comments: No marketing tags (GTM, GA, Hotjar, Segment, HubSpot, etc.) found in head.html or static HTML files. Analytics (Adobe Commerce Events SDK) is loaded via the delayed.js 3s timeout pattern, not in head. Clean head with no third-party marketing tags.

Checklist Item: User-facing strings in component render/template functions reference i18n keys or constants; hardcoded display strings >3 words flagged
Comments: Majority of user-facing strings use fetchPlaceholders() or labels?.* with ?? fallbacks (83 nullish-coalesce fallbacks, 65 placeholder usages). However, some hardcoded display strings found: 'Account Settings' (commerce-customer-information.js:36), 'Edit account settings and email preferences' and 'Buy directly from Canon Online Store' (commerce-my-account.js:58,79), 'Failed to load content.' (pdp-tabs.js:95). These should reference i18n keys.

Checklist Item: No binaries stored in repository
Comments: Fonts (.woff2 files) and one loader.gif are stored in the repository — acceptable and standard for web projects requiring self-hosted fonts. No media files (mp4/mov), executables (.exe), archives (.zip), or build artifacts stored in repo. Node_modules is excluded from consideration as it's typically gitignored.

Checklist Item: Error messages follow consistent pattern (checked via regex); no raw string throws; error constants or error classes used
Comments: Errors use raw string throws (throw new Error('...')). No error constants, error classes, or consistent prefix pattern enforced. console.error/warn messages have varying formats — some with context prefix like [Footer], most without. No structured error handling architecture. Acceptable for small/medium projects but not consistent.

Checklist Item: Avoid tight DOM coupling
Comments: Tight DOM coupling found in several blocks: renderAuthCombine.js queries #header-login-button, #popup-menu, .popupMenuContainer globally (cross-block dependency). product-list.js triggers .nav-dropdown-button click globally. login.js queries .login-content p by nth-of-type position. These blocks assume specific global DOM structure from other blocks. Enrichment.js queries .product-list-page globally which creates inter-block coupling.

Checklist Item: Avoid forced synchronous layouts
Comments: One forced synchronous layout found: product-details.js:1406 reads container.offsetHeight then immediately sets panel.style.height and appends panel to DOM — read-then-write in same synchronous call causes layout thrash. All other layout reads (getBoundingClientRect, scrollLeft, scrollHeight) occur inside event handlers (click, wheel, drag, resize) which run after the browser has already performed layout — acceptable pattern.

Checklist Item: All setTimeout/setInterval calls have corresponding clear calls; timer count per file within threshold (max 3); no timers <100ms
Comments: carousel.js has a setInterval (6000ms autoplay) with no corresponding clearInterval — potential memory leak if carousel block is removed from DOM. All setTimeout calls are UI state resets (200ms, 5000ms) — none sub-100ms. pdp-tabs properly clears its fetch abort timeout. Timer count per file is within threshold (max 2 per block). The uncleared carousel interval is the primary concern.

Checklist Item: setInterval for DOM/state checks flagged (suggest MutationObserver/IntersectionObserver/EventListener); polling intervals <1s flagged
Comments: Only one setInterval found: carousel autoplay at 6000ms interval (well above 1s threshold, not a DOM/state poll). The codebase uses modern observers extensively: 14 usages of MutationObserver, IntersectionObserver, or ResizeObserver for DOM/state detection. No polling intervals under 1s detected.
Evidence: blocks/carousel/carousel.js:76 (6000ms interval), blocks/ (14x modern observer usage)

Checklist Item: Prefer native browser APIs
Comments: No utility libraries (jQuery, lodash, moment, axios) found in block code. All DOM manipulation, event handling, fetch calls, and array operations use native browser APIs. The codebase is vanilla JS with native APIs only.

Checklist Item: Modern ES6+ syntax used
Comments: Modern ES6+ syntax used throughout. No var declarations, no prototype-based patterns, no .bind(this). 2344 instances of const/let, arrow functions, async/await, and ES modules (import/export). Optional chaining (?.), nullish coalescing (??), destructuring, template literals all used extensively.

Checklist Item: Avoid memory leaks
Comments: Memory leak risk: 104 addEventListener vs only 9 removeEventListener. Specific leaks: (1) product-details.js:926,932 adds window.mousemove and window.mouseup inside pill drag setup with no removeEventListener — leaks on every pill row init. (2) header.js:631 window.resize listener never removed. (3) header.js:690 window.focus listener never removed. header/renderAuthCombine.js and header.js do properly clean up keydown/focusout listeners. Carousel setInterval also not cleared (row 58).

Checklist Item: Event listeners cleaned up properly
Comments: Same finding as row 62. window.addEventListener calls for mousemove/mouseup (product-details.js), resize and focus (header.js) are never removed. Only 9 removeEventListener calls across 104 addEventListener. header/renderAuthCombine.js and pdp-tabs.js do clean up listeners properly. Majority of block-scoped element listeners are acceptable (element removal cleans them), but window-level listeners are leaked.

Checklist Item: Async logic uses async/await
Comments: Async/await used overwhelmingly throughout the codebase. Only 4 .then/.catch usages: product-list-page.js (2 .catch on async chains — acceptable), renderAuthDropdown.js (1 .then for a non-async context), containers.js (1 .catch on a fire-and-forget call). All are minor and justified. No promise chain nesting detected.
Evidence: blocks/ (grep .then/.catch — 4 hits vs thousands of async/await)

Checklist Item: Promises handled correctly
Comments: Promises well-handled: 27 try/catch blocks around async/await calls. async functions properly awaited. Promise.all/allSettled used for concurrent operations. Fire-and-forget .catch(console.error) used appropriately for non-critical operations like analytics. No unhandled promise rejections detected in source code patterns.

Checklist Item: State changes occur through defined setters/reducers/actions; no direct state object property mutations outside state management layer
Comments: No formal state management layer (Redux, signals, Zustand) — state is managed via module-level let variables mutated directly in functions. product-details.js has 8 module-level state variables (selectedMonths, cartLeaseTermLocked, selectedLeaseOptionId, etc.) mutated throughout. This is standard for EDS vanilla-JS blocks at this scale, but there are no setters/reducers — state changes happen inline. Acceptable for the project architecture.

Checklist Item: No mutation of shared state
Comments: No mutation of shared global state found in block code. Blocks use module-scoped let variables (not window properties). window.hlx, window.adobeDataLayer are framework-managed. No cross-block shared mutable objects detected. EDS event-bus is used for inter-block communication rather than shared mutable state.
Evidence: blocks/ (grep window.* = — no hits in block code)

Checklist Item: No side effects in pure functions
Comments: Functions with side effects are clearly named as actions (setLoading, updateStickyPrice, applyLeaseTerm, decorateX, buildX). Pure utility functions (formatCurrency, fmt, imageSlotConfig) are side-effect free. No functions disguised as pure that secretly mutate external state. The codebase follows an imperative style consistently — no mixed purity pattern violations detected.

Checklist Item: Consistent return types
Comments: Return types are reasonably consistent. 42 @returns JSDoc annotations document expected types. Utility functions consistently return typed values. Some event handlers return nothing (void) appropriately. No functions returning inconsistent types (e.g. sometimes HTMLElement, sometimes null without documentation) detected on inspection. No TypeScript enforcement but patterns are disciplined.

Checklist Item: No global variables
Comments: No ad-hoc global variables in block or script code. All blocks use ES module scope. Only intentional framework globals exist: window.hlx (EDS namespace), window.adobeDataLayer (Adobe analytics), window.isErrorPage/errorCode (set in static error pages). No var declarations at top level, no accidental global assignments detected.
Evidence: scripts/aem.js:18 (window.hlx framework namespace), blocks/ (no global var declarations)

Checklist Item: JS scoped to component or block
Comments: All blocks export a default decorate(block) function and operate within the block element's scope. ES module system ensures each block's JS is isolated. No shared state between blocks via module scope (only within the same block module). Fully compliant with EDS block architecture.

Checklist Item: Consistent coding style
Comments: Airbnb-base ESLint config enforced across the project. Key rules: import/extensions, linebreak-style, no-param-reassign (props allowed), no-use-before-define, no-console (warn/error allowed), no-unused-vars. ESLint passes clean on accordion, cards, carousel, header, and product-details blocks. Consistent coding style enforced by tooling.

Checklist Item: Avoid deeply nested callbacks
Comments: No deeply nested callbacks detected. Maximum indentation across all block files stays under 16 spaces (4 levels at 4-space indent). async/await eliminates callback nesting. Arrow functions used appropriately. Airbnb ESLint config max-depth rules would flag deep nesting. Code structure is flat and readable.
Evidence: blocks/ (awk indentation analysis — max 12 spaces/3 levels found)

Checklist Item: Call chain depth <=4 levels; wrapper functions that only delegate flagged; files with >80% re-exports flagged
Comments: No files with >80% re-exports found — only 3 files re-export constants. Call chain depth is hard to measure statically, but product-details.js at 1691 lines/46 functions likely has chains >4 levels in its decorate() orchestrator. No excessive wrapper delegation found. Re-export pattern is minimal and appropriate (constants barrel files).

Checklist Item: All external inputs are validated, sanitized, and securely handled to prevent errors, misuse, and security vulnerabilities
Comments: No raw end-user input interpolated into innerHTML. Dynamic innerHTML uses content-author data (readBlockConfig, nav content, labels from API) or sanitized API responses. URL params (returnRef) are used for API calls, not DOM injection. No eval(), no document.write(), no unsanitized user data in DOM. CSP nonce attribute used on all script tags in static pages.

Checklist Item: No unreachable code
Comments: ESLint no-unreachable rule run across all blocks JS — zero violations found. No code after return/throw/break statements detected.

Checklist Item: No empty catch blocks
Comments: No empty catch blocks in blocks/. ESLint no-empty rule shows zero violations. One intentional "do nothing" catch in scripts/scripts.js:137 for font loading (non-critical, explicitly commented). All other catch blocks log errors via console.error/console.warn.

Checklist Item: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance met
Comments: Full WCAG 2.1 AA automated test requires a running site (axe-cli/browser). Based on static code audit: known violations include tabindex=1 (row 3), missing fieldset/legend (row 6), radio inputs without labels (row 7), form errors not linked via aria-describedby (row 9). These are confirmed WCAG 2.1 AA failures. No a11y test tooling configured in package.json. Browser-based axe scan needed for complete picture.

Checklist Item: Announce dynamic content changes
Comments: Dynamic content announcements implemented for key interactions: cart/mini-cart update notifications use role="alert" + aria-live="assertive". Checkout credit application details use aria-live="polite". Cart button uses aria-busy for loading state. However, product list search results, PDP variant changes, and lease option updates do not appear to have live region announcements.

Checklist Item: Modal/dialog elements have role='dialog' or role='alertdialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby/aria-label; keydown handler for Escape key present in source; focusable elements exist inside dialog container
Comments: Mixed compliance: canon-shipping.js div dialogs correctly use role="dialog", aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby. product-details.js dialog (line 1682-1683) has aria-labelledby and aria-modal. However, modal.js (the shared modal utility) is missing aria-modal="true" and aria-labelledby — dialog has no accessible name. Native <dialog> handles Escape key natively. Focus management via MutationObserver focuses first input but skips buttons/links as initial focus.

Checklist Item: <video> elements have <track kind='captions'> with valid non-empty src
Comments: No video elements or video blocks found in the codebase. Not applicable.

Checklist Item: <audio> elements have adjacent transcript link or aria-describedby pointing to transcript content; transcript container visible (not display:none/visibility:hidden)
Comments: No audio elements found in the codebase. Not applicable.

Checklist Item: No focus-trap calls outside of modal/dialog components; no keydown handlers that programmatically restrict Tab key to a subset of elements on non-modal pages
Comments: Focus trap in renderAuthCombine.js is correctly scoped to the auth modal overlay (signInModal). It traps Tab within focusable modal elements and handles Escape to close. Listener is properly removed on close (removeEventListener). No focus trapping outside of modal/dialog contexts found.

Checklist Item: Visible focus styles present
Comments: Focus styles generally present. :focus/:focus-visible styles defined for accordion summary, carousel buttons, columns links, PDP tabs, checkout inputs, and login inputs. Carousel dots use outline:0 but offset with :focus-visible rule at line 117. Checkout select removes outline but provides :focus border styling. header.css outline:none is on a non-focusable dropdown list container. Global button focus style in styles.css changes background color. No critical focus-style removal on interactive elements without replacement.

Checklist Item: All CSS :hover rules have corresponding :focus or :focus-visible rules on the same selectors
Comments: product-details.css has 12 :hover rules and zero :focus/:focus-visible counterparts — all hover state changes (underline, color, cursor) are inaccessible to keyboard users. Other blocks partially comply: carousel, columns, pdp-tabs, checkout all have :focus or :focus-visible rules alongside :hover. This is a gap specifically in product-details.css.

Checklist Item: Visually-hidden patterns (.sr-only/.visually-hidden) use approved clip/position technique; no display:none or visibility:hidden on screen-reader-targeted text
Comments: No .sr-only/.visually-hidden utility class defined in this project. The only visually-hidden pattern used is text-indent:-9999px on carousel indicator buttons, which is an older but functional technique — text is hidden visually but accessible to screen readers. Buttons also have aria-label for accessible names. No display:none or visibility:hidden on screen-reader-targeted text found.

Checklist Item: No eval() or Function(string) usage
Comments: No eval() or new Function(string) usage in custom block or script code. The only occurrence is inside the minified third-party commerce-events-sdk.js (Adobe Analytics SDK) which is a vendored library outside the team's control. Zero violations in custom codebase.

Checklist Item: No document.write or document.writeln
Comments: No document.write() or document.writeln() found in any custom code. Zero violations.

Checklist Item: External scripts use Subresource Integrity (SRI) where applicable
Comments: SRI partially implemented: RUM enhancer script uses conditional integrity hash when provided. However, Verint script (verint-script.js) and dynamically-loaded third-party scripts via loadScript() do not use SRI. Static HTML pages have no external CDN script tags. The loadScript() utility supports an attrs parameter that could accept integrity but no callers currently use it for third-party scripts.

Checklist Item: No unsanitized user input in innerHTML or dangerous sinks
Comments: Already verified in row 75. No raw user input interpolated into innerHTML or other dangerous DOM sinks. All innerHTML values come from content-author data (readBlockConfig), i18n labels, or API product data. URL params (returnRef) used for API calls only, not DOM injection. No eval(), no document.write(). CSP nonce enforced on all static page scripts.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in source
Comments: No hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets found in custom code. Configuration values (store IDs, environment names) are loaded via getConfigValue() from external config JSON, not hardcoded. Token handling uses cookie-based auth (getUserTokenCookie). No OpenAI/AWS/Stripe/GitHub credential patterns detected.
Evidence: scripts/commerce.js (getConfigValue pattern), blocks/ (0 credential pattern hits)

Checklist Item: No credentials or tokens in URLs or query parameters
Comments: No credentials, tokens, or secrets passed as URL query parameters found in custom code. API calls use Authorization headers via the Adobe Commerce GraphQL pattern (Bearer tokens in headers, not URLs). No token= or key= query params in fetch calls.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data in client-side comments or console logs
Comments: No console.log calls in block code. Only console.debug (2 instances for non-sensitive error messages), console.error and console.warn for diagnostics. No sensitive data (tokens, PII, credentials) in log messages or source comments. Comments are descriptive technical notes only.

Checklist Item: No insecure storage usage (localStorage/sessionStorage/Cookies or other browser storage for secrets)
Comments: localStorage used for non-sensitive behavioral data (product view/purchase history). sessionStorage used for temporary checkout address cache (PII — shipping/billing addresses) during active checkout session, cleared with removeItem after use. No auth tokens, passwords, or credentials stored in localStorage/sessionStorage. Auth tokens managed via cookies (getUserTokenCookie pattern). The address caching in sessionStorage is a common and acceptable checkout UX pattern.

Checklist Item: No PII stored in cookies or browser storage without encryption or consent
Comments: Consent management is a stub: getConsent() always returns true with a console.warn('getConsent not implemented'). This means behavioral data (product view history, purchase history in localStorage) and analytics are collected without actual consent verification. Address data in sessionStorage during checkout is transient and session-scoped, but the broader consent system is unimplemented. This is a compliance risk (GDPR/CCPA).

Checklist Item: No known vulnerable dependencies (npm audit / Snyk)
Comments: npm audit shows 35 total vulnerabilities: 5 critical, 13 high, 17 moderate. All are transitive (indirect) dependencies — none are direct dependencies. Critical: @adobe/aep-rules-engine, @adobe/alloy, @vitest/coverage-v8, basic-ftp, vitest. High includes js-cookie (runtime), vite, rollup (build-time). Most critical/high are in dev/build tooling not shipped to production, but js-cookie and @adobe/alloy are runtime concerns. No npm audit configuration or CI audit gate found.
Evidence: npm audit output: 5 critical, 13 high, all indirect

Checklist Item: No sensitive data exposed in client-visible IDs or data attributes
Comments: All data-* attributes contain non-sensitive UI state: slide indices, loading flags, step numbers, option IDs, label text. No PII, tokens, auth data, or sensitive identifiers in data attributes. Element IDs are functional (nav, carousel-0, tab-0) without exposing sensitive data.
Evidence: blocks/ (grep dataset.* — all non-sensitive UI state values)

Comments: ESLint run across all blocks JS with --max-warnings 0 returns zero errors and zero warnings. Stylelint run across all blocks CSS returns zero errors. Both linters pass cleanly.
Evidence: npx eslint blocks/ (0 errors), npx stylelint blocks/**/*.css (0 errors)

Comments: ESLint no-unused-vars scan across all blocks found only 1 occurrence: '_cart' (prefixed with underscore to indicate intentional non-use). Convention-compliant. Zero true unused variables found.
Evidence: npx eslint blocks/ --rule no-unused-vars (1 hit: _cart, underscore-prefixed intentional)

Checklist Item: No unused functions
Comments: ESLint no-unused-vars scan for functions shows zero violations beyond the single underscore-prefixed _cart variable (row 99). No declared-but-uncalled functions found in block code.
Evidence: npx eslint blocks/ --rule no-unused-vars (0 function hits)

Comments: No unused imports detected by ESLint across all blocks. All imported identifiers are actively used in their respective files.

Comments: ESLint no-unreachable and no-constant-condition checks across all blocks return zero violations. No dead code paths or always-false conditionals detected.

Comments: All block sub-modules are referenced: containers.js (54 refs), fragments.js (94 refs), utils.js (35 refs), constants.js (14 refs), canon-shipping.js (1), lease-section.js (1), renderAuthCombine.js (1), renderAuthDropdown.js (1). No dead/unreferenced module files found.

Checklist Item: No commented-out production code
Comments: 4 commented-out event registrations found in commerce-checkout.js:492-495: events.on('checkout/updated'), events.on('checkout/values'), events.on('order/placed'), events.on('cart/initialized'). These appear to be temporarily disabled production code paths. Should be removed or replaced with TODO comments explaining why they are disabled.

Checklist Item: No implicit globals
Comments: No implicit globals in block code. All code uses ES module scope. ESLint no-undef with browser+es2022 env shows zero real violations — only false positives for standard browser APIs (Promise, Map, Set etc.) which are expected globals. No accidental global variable leaks detected.
Evidence: npx eslint blocks/ --rule no-undef --env browser,es2022 (0 real violations)

Checklist Item: Consistent file structure
Comments: Block structure is consistent: each block has a blockname.js and blockname.css. One exception: blocks/signin-split/ is an empty directory with no JS or CSS files — likely a leftover placeholder that should be removed.

Checklist Item: No duplicated constants
Comments: No duplicated constant names found across block files. Shared constants are centralized in scripts/constants.js and blocks/product-details/constants.js which re-exports from the central file. No same-value string/number literals assigned to multiple constants detected.

Checklist Item: Variable names >2 characters (excluding loop iterators i/j/k/x/y); no single-letter variables outside loops; no anti-pattern names (temp/tmp/data/val/stuff/thing/foo/bar without qualifier); camelCase or snake_case consistent per file
Comments: Naming is predominantly good: camelCase throughout, descriptive names used. Minor findings: `tmp` used 3× as a temp DOM element (acceptable short scope), `data` used as fetch result name (conventional), `val` used once in a clear context. No anti-patterns like foo/bar/stuff found. No single-letter variables outside loops. Consistent camelCase per file.

Checklist Item: Function names start with verb (get/set/is/has/should/create/update/delete/handle/render/fetch/parse/validate/transform/format/check/find/load/save/remove/add/init/build/compute/on); names >4 characters; no generic names (doStuff/handleIt/process/run without descriptive suffix)
Comments: Function names are predominantly verb-prefixed and descriptive: build*, create*, decorate*, handle*, apply*, attach*, load*, close*, open*, fetch*, etc. Minor exceptions: imageSlotConfig (config builder, not verb-prefixed), shuffleArray/serializeSort/serializeFilter (descriptive compound nouns, acceptable). No generic names (doStuff/process/run without qualifier) found. All names >4 characters.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Consistent camelCase for JS variables/functions, BEM/kebab-case for CSS classes. One exception: 'authCombineNavElement' camelCase class name in renderAuthCombine.js (noted in row 32). Constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. File names use kebab-case. Overall naming consistency is high.

Checklist Item: No nested ternary expressions beyond 1 level; no if/else chains >4 branches (suggest switch/object-map); boolean expressions <=3 conditions per statement; complex boolean expressions without intermediate named variable flagged
Comments: ESLint no-nested-ternary reports zero violations across all blocks. No nested ternaries found. No if/else chains >4 branches detected on grep review. Boolean expressions are concise throughout.

Checklist Item: Functions with if-block wrapping >80% of function body flagged for guard-clause inversion; nested if-else >3 levels deep where outermost condition is validation/type-check flagged for early return
Comments: Codebase uses 80 early-return guard clauses actively. No deeply nested if-else structures found (no 10+ spaces of indentation in largest files). Guard-clause pattern is well-adopted throughout blocks.
Evidence: blocks/ (80 early-return guards found, no deep nesting in product-details/commerce-checkout)

Checklist Item: Average file size under 500 LOC; functions under 40 lines; module boundaries well-defined
Comments: product-details.js at 1691 lines significantly exceeds the 500 LOC guideline and has many functions over 40 lines. containers.js (781), header.js (706), canon-shipping.js (664) also exceed 500 LOC. Average file size across all blocks is ~247 lines (acceptable). The product-details block lacks module decomposition — the 1691-line file should be split into sub-modules similar to the commerce-checkout pattern.

Checklist Item: Files have single responsibility and clear module boundaries
Comments: Most blocks have clear single responsibility. commerce-checkout is well-decomposed into sub-modules (commerce-checkout.js, containers.js, canon-shipping.js, fragments.js, utils.js, lease-section.js). However, product-details.js at 1691 lines handles PDP layout, lease options, print plans, comparison modal, breadcrumbs, variant selection, sticky bar, and dark bar — multiple responsibilities in one file without sub-module decomposition.

Checklist Item: README present
Comments: Root README.md present. 44 out of ~49 block directories have README.md files. Missing READMEs: accordion, enrichment, fragment, signin-split (empty dir), targeted-block. Core commerce blocks (product-details, checkout, cart, header, footer etc.) are all documented.

Checklist Item: Consistent error handling strategy
Comments: 24 catch blocks across blocks. Strategy is consistent: most log via console.error/console.warn with context. One intentional silent catch in product-details.js:278 with /* ignore */ comment for a JSON-LD parse fallback (acceptable). No inconsistent error suppression. Error handling is not centralized but is applied uniformly.
Evidence: blocks/ (24 catch blocks reviewed), blocks/product-details/product-details.js:278 (intentional silent catch)

Checklist Item: Errors logged meaningfully
Comments: All console.error and console.warn calls include descriptive context strings before the error object. Examples: 'Error opening mini PDP modal:', 'Error fetching products by SKU:', '[Footer] Error loading external footer:', 'Failed to fetch product variants:'. No bare console.error(e) without context found.
Evidence: blocks/ (grep console.error/warn — all include context strings)

Checklist Item: Errors fail gracefully
Comments: Graceful error handling implemented at multiple levels: commerce init failure redirects to 418 error page, PDP uses showError() inline alert component for API/cart errors, form validation shows inline field-level error spans. pdp-tabs shows 'Failed to load content' message in tab panel. Product list shows inline error on search failure. Errors do not crash the page.

Checklist Item: Avoid magic numbers
Comments: 19 magic numbers found. Notable: 6000 (carousel autoplay interval), 5000 (notification auto-dismiss × 2), 3000 (scripts.js delayed import timeout), 900 (mobile breakpoint), 10000 (pdp-tabs fetch abort timeout). These should be named constants (e.g. CAROUSEL_AUTOPLAY_INTERVAL = 6000). Most are benign in context but reduce maintainability.

Checklist Item: Constants centralized
Comments: Central scripts/constants.js exists for shared cross-block constants (OPTION_ID_LEASE, OPTION_PRINT_PLAN, EVENT_PRODUCT_RAW). 64 additional block-scoped constants are defined locally in their respective blocks — appropriate since they are not shared. commerce-checkout has its own constants.js for checkout-specific values. Constants are reasonably centralized at the appropriate level.

Checklist Item: Config-driven behavior
Comments: Config-driven behavior well implemented: 34 usages of getConfigValue() (commerce settings), getMetadata() (page-level flags like no-header, auth, root), and readBlockConfig() (block-level author config). Commerce URLs, store IDs, analytics settings all driven by external config.json. Footer URL, block variants, and auth requirements are all configurable.

Checklist Item: Shared utilities extracted
Comments: Shared utilities are well extracted. scripts/utils/ contains auth.js, cart-utils.js, graphql-service.js, helpers.js, external-asset-utils.js, constants.js. scripts/components/ contains reusable UI components: inline-alert, commerce-cart-modal, commerce-mini-pdp, cart-accordion, scoped-spinner. scripts/initializers/ handles shared init logic. Good utility extraction pattern followed.

Checklist Item: Duplicate code percentage within standard threshold
Comments: jscpd analysis: 17 clone pairs detected with total duplication of 1.87% tokens / 2.06% lines across all blocks. Well within the acceptable <5% threshold. The codebase has good code reuse through shared utilities.
Evidence: npx jscpd blocks/ (1.87% token duplication, 17 clones)

Checklist Item: No hardcoded environment values
Comments: footer.js hardcodes EXTERNAL_FOOTER_URL = 'https://www.usa.canon.com/...' as a production environment constant. This should be driven by config/metadata so it can vary per environment. All other environment values (commerce endpoints, store IDs, analytics config) are correctly loaded via getConfigValue(). This is the only hardcoded environment URL found in block code.

Checklist Item: Avoid circular dependencies
Comments: 10 circular dependencies detected by madge. Key cycles: commerce.js ↔ initializers/index.js (and its children auth.js, cart.js, personalization.js), commerce.js ↔ initializers/pdp.js, scripts.js ↔ fragment/fragment.js, header.js ↔ header/renderAuthDropdown.js. These can cause load-order issues and are a maintainability risk. The initializer pattern creates a mutual dependency with the commerce module.
Evidence: npx madge blocks/ --circular (10 cycles: commerce.js/initializers, scripts.js/fragment, header/renderAuthDropdown)

Checklist Item: No stale TODO/FIXME comments; no comments referencing symbols that do not exist in scope; no commented-out code blocks
Comments: No TODO, FIXME, HACK, or XXX comments found in any block or script code. The only commented-out code is the 4 event handler registrations in commerce-checkout.js:492-495 (already flagged in row 104). No stale symbol references in comments detected.

Checklist Item: commented-out code blocks >3 lines flagged; TODO/FIXME without issue-tracker reference flagged;
Comments: Same findings as row 126. commerce-checkout.js:492-495 has 4 consecutive commented-out event registrations (>3 lines). No TODO/FIXME comments exist anywhere in custom code to flag for issue-tracker references.

Checklist Item: Charset meta tag (<meta charset="UTF-8">) declared in <head> before any content-producing elements; charset declaration appears within first 1024 bytes of document
Comments: No <meta charset="UTF-8"> found in head.html, 404.html, or 418.html. The charset declaration is absent from all production HTML files. EDS may set charset via HTTP Content-Type header, but the HTML meta declaration is still required per spec to be within the first 1024 bytes as a safety fallback. This is a critical omission.

Checklist Item: Language attribute (lang) on <html> element is a valid BCP 47 tag matching page content language; lang attribute not empty or set to 'undefined'
Comments: lang="en" set via scripts.js:108 (document.documentElement.lang = 'en') during loadEager() — valid BCP 47 tag. However, 404.html and 418.html have <html> without lang attribute in markup (only set at runtime by scripts.js). Static error pages should have lang="en" directly in HTML for cases where JS fails. EDS-served pages get lang set by scripts.js before content renders.
Evidence: scripts/scripts.js:108 (lang set in JS), 404.html / 418.html (lang absent in HTML markup)

Checklist Item: Direction attribute (dir) specified on <html> or relevant container elements for RTL language pages; value is 'ltr' or 'rtl' matching content language direction
Comments: No dir attribute found. Site is English-only (lang="en"), LTR direction is implicit and does not require explicit dir="ltr". Not applicable unless RTL language support is added in the future.
Evidence: N/A — English-only site, no RTL support needed

Checklist Item: If CSS print stylesheet (@media print) is defined, print styles hide non-essential UI elements (navigation/footer/sidebar/ads)
Comments: No @media print styles or print.css found in the project. Not applicable.

Checklist Item: Font-family declarations defined at :root or body level using CSS custom properties; component-level stylesheets reference font-family tokens instead of hardcoding font names

No hardcoded font names (Arial, Roboto, sans-serif etc.) found in any block CSS. Full compliance with font token usage.
Evidence: styles/styles.css:169-172 (token definitions), blocks/ (no hardcoded font-family values)

Checklist Item: Content elements have explicit width and height HTML attributes or CSS aspect-ratio set to reserve layout space and prevent CLS
Comments: Mixed compliance: product-list.js includes width="300" height="225" on img elements (correct). lease-options card images are in a fixed 60×60px CSS container (CLS controlled). lease-estimator-modal img lacks explicit dimensions and only has max-width/max-height with auto — potential CLS. commerce-my-account cartImg lacks width/height attributes. EDS createOptimizedPicture() generates width/height attributes on img elements within picture elements.

Checklist Item: No unused npm/yarn packages in package.json; depcheck or npm-check analysis confirms all listed dependencies are imported in source code
Comments: depcheck reports 3 "unused" runtime deps (@adobe/adobe-client-data-layer, event-collector, events-sdk) but these are pre-bundled into scripts/acdl/ and scripts/commerce-events-*.js — not imported via ES module syntax, hence depcheck false-positives. stylelint-config-standard is used in .stylelintrc.json.
